entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 18 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 18 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-18 04:22AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: Cameron Smith wins 2022 British Open; rising interest rates to cost taxpayers an extra $13m
  • Victoria Police payout for Age photographer pepper-sprayed at protest
  • My colleagues and I wished the office was less boring. Careful what you wish for
  • The moments that changed these dancers’ lives – and brought them here
  • WA tips over a million COVID-19 cases as hospital admissions surge
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Studio 54 owner chose assisted suicide
  • The ‘unknown’ who sang for Kylie, warmed up for Queen and backed James Brown
  • Nerves of steel: Kennedy overcomes horror start to win pole vault bronze at worlds
  • NSW schools to begin four-week COVID-19 ‘blitz’ as term 3 begins
  • ASX trades higher behind energy stocks after Wall Street rally
  • German woman, 20, killed, man, 19, injured in northern beaches boat crash
  • Open triumph would be stained if Cameron Smith makes Saudi switch
  • Gamble pays off as Melbourne United star signs with Denver Nuggets
  • Best of cartoons, July 18, 2022
  • Australia can safely improve its relations with China. Here’s how
  • Pandemic leave reversal is a teachable moment for Albanese
  • Rising interest rates to cost taxpayers an extra $13 billion
  • Best of cartoons, July 17, 2022
  • ‘Creeping politicisation’: One in five federal board roles held by political appointees
  • Are ‘box tickers’ armed with DNA tests inflating the Indigenous population?

  • Sydney’s CBD to remain scooter-free after council rejects trial
  • Not a backflip, but a sign of PM who listens and acts
  • From the Archives, 1982: When the dealer shows his hand
  • In the Herald: July 18, 1969
  • Mary’s duo to open Sydney’s newest live music venue
  • Youth Koori Court expanded under $20m Indigenous justice package
  • Jinx testing in progress

  • James Webb offers new perspective on something closer to home: Jupiter
  • Flames reach the beach as heat and wildfires rage across Europe
  • Nearly 400 police on scene but ‘egregiously poor’ decisions: Uvalde report
  • ‘Like leaving doors of the Louvre open’: New fee for Venice day-trippers
  • Zelensky fires top officials over agency collaboration with Russians
  • Inside the Ukraine war crimes investigation, Part 3: The resistance
  • Britain should try to build Australia’s first nuclear subs: Andrew Hastie
  • No mustard! France stews without must-have condiment
  • As Ireland grows less benighted, America turns cruel for women
  • Ukrainian cargo plane carrying ‘dangerous material’ crashes in Greece
  • New Zealand extends fuel excise cut until next year in bid to help rising cost of living
  • V/Line running clunkers on long-distance trips ‘excessively’
  • Roberts-Smith entitled to presumption of innocence, court told
  • Queensland COVID modelling ‘just a couple of charts’ says premier
  • Boy dies in hospital after falling off e-scooter
  • How Melbourne’s wealth is shifting outwards from the city centre
  • ‘Mask up for people like me’: Cancer fighter takes shelter as viruses rampage through Perth
  • Former senator splurges on taxpayer dime
  • Woolies forks out $150m for targeted ad company Shopper Media
  • Flatlining: China’s economy has been crippled by its zero-COVID policies
  • ANZ to buy Suncorp’s banking arm for $4.9 billion
  • Education key for novice investors
  • ‘Come back when you’re grey’: Young people warned against super switching
  • Silver linings: The surprising way the pandemic has helped women at work
  • Ladbrokes owner launches live racing channels in media push
  • Australians spent $8 billion on streaming and gaming in 2021: PwC
  • ‘Looking at opportunities everywhere’: Rio Tinto on the hunt for more lithium assets
  • Here’s proof that venture capitalists don’t really know what they’re doing
  • ‘Makes more sense’: Investors back ANZ play for Suncorp assets as MYOB deal put on ice
  • Quick Q: Andy Marinos’ love of sport, family and Andrea Bocelli
  • Celebrities play their strangest roles yet in new portrait show
  • A century after Kerouac’s birth, will it take me another 100 years to finish On The Road?
  • Meet Jennifer Affleck: J Lo and Ben Affleck married in secret Las Vegas wedding
  • The sauciest show on TV: Netflix’s latest offering will transform your sex life
  • ‘The only way forward’: the bullet-shaped canoes that tell the story of two wars
  • The chance encounter that helped send Indigenous art rippling through the US
  • Is the world’s biggest library full of stolen content?
  • Looking for a TV upgrade? Put this model top of your list
  • Netflix’s next challenge: piracy is back
  • Could a bigger dam save Sydney from flooding? The question confronting the city
  • From mussels to ancient fish, WA freshwater species plummet
  • Inside the war room where the fight against the varroa mite is raging
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 18 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
